name: German Chocolate Cake with Coconut-Pecan Frosting
recipeIngredient: 4 ounces sweet cooking chocolate, cut into pieces
{or 1 tablespoon vinegar or lemon juice, plus enough milk to make 1 cup}
recipeInstructions:
1. Preheat oven to 350°F (175°C). Grease and lightly flour three 9-inch round cake pans.
- In a small saucepan over low heat, melt chocolate with water; let cool.
2. In a large bowl, combine 2 cups sugar and 1 cup butter or margarine. Beat until light and fluffy.
- Add 4 eggs, one at a time, beating well after each addition.
- Stir in the cooled chocolate mixture.
- Gradually add all remaining cake ingredients; beat at low speed until well combined.
- Pour batter into prepared pans.
3. Bake for 35 to 45 minutes or until a toothpick inserted in the center comes out clean.
- Let cool for 5 minutes, then remove from pans.
- Allow cakes to cool completely for about an hour.
4. In a medium saucepan, combine 1 cup sugar, evaporated milk, 1/2 cup butter or margarine, and 3 eggs.
- Mix well and cook over medium heat until the mixture begins to bubble, stirring constantly.
- Remove from heat and stir in coconut, pecans, and vanilla.
- Let cool completely.
5. Place one cake layer on a serving plate with the top side down. Spread about 1/3 of the frosting.
- Repeat with remaining layers and frosting, finishing with frosting on top.
